subject: Cloud Computing
title: Bandwidth Bills That Shock
description: A 60-second lesson on Bandwidth Bills That Shock.

hook.text: A startup's app went viral. Millions of users downloaded data. The bandwidth bill was bigger than the server bill.

buildup.text: Data transfer costs are the most underestimated line item in cloud bills. They grow with every user.

discovery.text: CDNs reduce bandwidth costs by caching content at edge locations closer to users. Less data leaves the cloud.

twist.text: Multi-region architectures multiply bandwidth costs. Data replicating between regions racks up charges fast.

climax.text: Cloudflare built a business by offering zero egress fees. The industry is slowly being forced to compete.

punchline.text: Users love your app. Your bandwidth bill loves them more.

quiz.question: How do CDNs help reduce cloud bandwidth costs?
options: By caching content at edge locations so less data leaves the cloud
By compressing all data to one-tenth its original size
By blocking users from downloading any content
